golden.answer: A memo is a small optional field attached to a Stellar transaction. It can carry text, an ID, a hash, or a return hash; the common consumer use is an exchange or other pooled service telling you which memo to include so it can credit your account. A memo is not a password and does not prove who owns an address. Use exactly the type and value supplied by the receiving service.
keyFacts: Defines a memo as transaction metadata with several types.
Explains the pooled-service crediting use and exact supplied value.
avoid: Do NOT call a memo a password or private key.
Do NOT tell users to invent a memo for an exchange deposit.

corroboration.claim: Stellar supports memo types and pooled services use memos to identify a customer deposit.

verified.by: P4 H3 primary+blind (Solo 3415/3419) with Fable voice review
